marketState: neutral with a cautious bullish tilt — hot weather demand and a crowded managed-money short offset by a storage surplus and bearish daily/weekly trend
summary: Spot is trading right at fair value ($2.911 vs $2.923 fair) and below its 16-year seasonal median (-7.7 pp), with seasonality reliability low this week and 1-4 week seasonal odds mixed (44/56/38/38%). The standout signal is weather: next-7-day demand is +11.5% vs the 5y norm and the 8-16 day outlook is +29.1% vs 5y and above the historical max range, an unusually strong cooling-demand pulse. Fair value is drifting higher through time ($2.923 now to $3.081 by +21D) while spot stays flat, leaving price slightly under fair by day 21, and managed money is net short at just the 8th percentile of 5 years — a crowded short that raises squeeze risk. Working against that, storage sits +180 Bcf above the 5y average with a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise, and daily/weekly technicals remain in a bearish trend (RSI ~37, MACD negative, 50<200 EMA) even though the futures curve shows only mild front-month backwardation before a normal winter contango. Net: fundamentals lean bullish over the coming weeks while the storage overhang and daily/weekly downtrend argue for patience near-term.

longTerm.rationale: Fair value climbs from $2.923 now to $3.081 by +21D while spot is flat, and managed money is net short at just the 8th 5y percentile — a crowded short vulnerable to covering. 8-16 day weather demand is +29% vs 5y and above the historical max, supporting firmer gas demand heading into the seasonally strongest month (Sep).
longTerm.invalidation: A close below $2.70 alongside continued bearish storage surprises and weather demand reverting toward/below the 5y norm would invalidate the long thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: Front price is already fairly priced now (spread -$0.012, status FAIR), so there's no near-term mispricing edge, and technicals conflict: 15m/1h lean neutral-to-bullish (RSI 52-58) while 4h/1d/1w trend is bearish (RSI ~37-49, 50<200 EMA). The curve shows only mild front backwardation (-$0.035 to Sep), giving no strong directional tilt despite hot 1-7D weather (+11.5% vs 5y).
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a daily close above ~$3.00 with the 4h EMA flipping 50>200 to confirm a bullish reversal (long entry), or a break/close below ~$2.80 support with MACD staying negative (short entry).

keyDrivers: 8-16D weather demand +29.1% vs 5y and above the historical max — strong cooling-demand pulse
Managed money net short at the 8th 5y percentile — crowded short, squeeze risk
Fair value rising from $2.923 (now) to $3.081 (+21D) while spot price is flat
Storage +180 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise
Daily/weekly technicals in a bearish trend (RSI ~37, MACD negative, 50<200 EMA) despite oversold readings
risks: Seasonality reliability is Low this week (59 pp 10-90 spread) — the seasonal edge is unreliable
